spec_match.rationale: The concept successfully simulates how SVG filters were used to leak pixel data via color matrix transformations, demonstrating the exact security threat.

interactive.rationale: The interactive filter pills and 'Sample pixels' canvas readout provide a highly educational, hands-on attack simulation.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Directly maps to the security sidechannel concern referenced in the feature's main description.

edge_cases.rationale: Simulates multiple complex filter styles (matrix shift and displacement) to show how different sidechannel signatures work.

references.rationale: Cites the correct ChromeStatus entry and cross-links to sibling clickjacking/sandbox demos.

design_system.rationale: Uses native focusable buttons for filter controls, matches standard CSS variables, and follows AA color contrast guidelines.

openQuestions.title: Add ARIA roles for active state of filter pills
openQuestions.detail: Add `aria-pressed="true/false"` to the filter pills (<button class="filter-pill">) to reflect the selected active filter state semantically for screen-reader users, similar to the buttons in the filter-comparison demo.

summary: This is an exceptional educational demo that beautifully simulates the precise sidechannel pixel leak attack vector, proving the high security value of the Chrome 150 SVG filter boundary rule.
